utility_evaluation.REASONING: There are 5 items in the required format: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. In the final plan: health_coverage is present with type and costs, regions covered (meets physical/mental unspecified but has costs); property_coverage is null (missing and thus not covered); travel_coverage is present but lacks explicit costs (required to include costs); additional_services are listed with costs (covered); total_budget is present with monthly and annual totals (covered). Fully specified according to the format means each must include the details specified: health_coverage should include costs (present), property_coverage should include home/contents insurance with costs (missing), travel_coverage should include travel insurance with costs (costs missing), additional_services with costs (present), total_budget with complete cost breakdown (present). Thus, 3 items fully specified out of 5.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan lists a generic 'Comprehensive International' health coverage and includes travel coverage within it, plus two additional services: digital_health_management and chronic_condition_tracking. The ground-truth ratings catalog provides specific named plans (e.g., PrimeCare Premium Plan, EliteCare Premium Plan) and a set of health/travel/property enhancement services with defined names and ratings. The items in the final plan do not match any named options in the ground-truth. Therefore, there is no direct item-to-rating mapping available from the provided ratings, since none of the final plan item names appear in the ground-truth list.
